content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size KKK.

Note that 1≤A1<A2<…<AK=N1\le A_1 \lt A_2 \lt \ldots \lt A_K = N1≤A1​<A2​<…<AK​=N and K≤NK \le NK≤N.

Consider an array BBB of size NNN. We define the prefix maximum array of BBB as an array consisting of **all** BiB_iBi​ (in **order**), such that Bi>BjB_i \gt B_jBi​>Bj​ for all j<ij \lt ij<i.

Your task is to find the number of permutations PPP of length NNN such that AAA is the prefix maximum array of PPP.

Since this number might be huge, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

Note:


A permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once.
For the permutation P=[2,1,4,3]P = [2, 1, 4, 3]P=[2,1,4,3], the prefix maximum array is [2,4][2, 4][2,4], since the elements A1A_1A1​ and A3A_3A3​ satisfy the given condition.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the size of the permutation and the size of the prefix maximum array, respectively.
The next line contains KKK space-separated integers A1,A2,…,AKA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_KA1​,A2​,…,AK​ denoting the prefix maximum array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of permutations of length NNN having the prefix maximum array AAA, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Here N=4N = 4N=4 and prefix maximum array is [2,4][2, 4][2,4]. The possible permutations are [2,1,4,3][2, 1, 4, 3][2,1,4,3], [2,4,1,3][2, 4, 1, 3][2,4,1,3], and [2,4,3,1][2, 4, 3, 1][2,4,3,1].
Test case 222: Here N=3N = 3N=3 and prefix maximum array is [3][3][3]. The possible permutations are [3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2] and [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1].
